opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Everyone is mumbling and ignoring me on purpose, and my ears are ringing constantly! (Spouse on the call: he can't hear a word anyone says anymore, he has the TV blaring, and tonight he's convinced we're all whispering behind his back — it's tearing us apart.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The spouse called after hours because the patient became very agitated tonight, insisting the family is deliberately excluding him, and they wanted urgent help settling him and working out why he can't understand anyone.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual, coming on slowly over about the last five years, per the spouse
symptom_narrative.duration: years, but reaching a head tonight with the agitation
symptom_narrative.character: trouble making out words even though he can hear that people are talking, especially high-pitched voices; constant ringing in both ears
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the spouse says it has got bad enough that they can barely hold a conversation and it caused a big argument tonight
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time; much worse when there is background noise or more than one person talking
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have found; he turns the TV right up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: noisy or crowded rooms, several people talking at once, talking to him from another room
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: constant high-pitched ringing in both ears; frequently mishears the grandchildren; increasingly avoids family gatherings and has withdrawn socially; became upset and suspicious tonight that people were whispering to leave him out
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no sudden episodes described; the spouse says it has just slowly crept up over years
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: physically independent and gets around fine, but conversation and family relationships are badly affected and he has been withdrawing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: spouse reports his hearing has been slowly getting worse for years but he has never had it formally checked

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications reported; spouse says he has not started any new tablets recently

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with his spouse; used to be sociable but has been pulling away from family and gatherings because he cannot follow conversations; the communication breakdown is causing significant marital tension

communication_style.typical_message_style: speaks very loudly, becomes defensive and can misinterpret questions; the spouse frequently steps in to relay and clarify

access_barriers.other: profound difficulty hearing over the phone means much of the exchange is carer-relayed

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports he has the television turned up to a very loud, disruptive volume and constantly asks people to repeat themselves
spouse reports he mishears the grandchildren and has been withdrawing from family gatherings over recent months
on the call he is speaking very loudly, appears agitated and defensive, and misinterprets several questions
spouse states he denies any ear pain, any sudden pop or blockage, and there has been no recent injury
